title: Make P the subject of I = Prt
problem: The simple interest formula is $I = Prt$, where $I$ is interest earned, $P$ is the principal, $r$ is the annual interest rate and $t$ is time in years. Make $P$ the subject. Find $P$ when $I = \$180$, $r = 0.04$ and $t = 3$.

steps.instruction: Identify what is being done to $P$ in the formula.
steps.working: In $I = Prt$, $P$ is multiplied by $r$ and then by $t$. To isolate $P$, divide both sides by $rt$.

title: Make r the subject of A = pi r squared
problem: The area of a circle is $A = \pi r^2$, where $r > 0$. Make $r$ the subject. Find $r$ when $A = 153.94\,\text{cm}^2$. Use $\pi = 3.14159$.

steps.instruction: Identify what is being done to $r$.
steps.working: In $A = \pi r^2$, $r$ is first squared and then multiplied by $\pi$. Reverse the operations in the opposite order.

steps.instruction: Take the positive square root of both sides (since $r > 0$).

title: Make h the subject of SA = 2pi r(r + h)
problem: The total surface area of a closed cylinder is $SA = 2\pi r(r + h)$. Make $h$ the subject. Find $h$ when $SA = 376.99\,\text{cm}^2$ and $r = 4\,\text{cm}$. Use $\pi = 3.14159$.

steps.instruction: Divide both sides by $2\pi r$ to isolate the bracket containing $h$.
Subtract $r$ from both sides to make $h$ the subject.

steps.working:
$$h = 15.0 - 4 = 11\,\text{cm}$$

The cylinder has a height of $11\,\text{cm}$.
